div1.entryFree: Trick, vb. to dress out, to adorn: in the phrase of war, which they t. up with new-tuned oaths, H5 III, 6, 80. head to foot now is he total gules, horridly --ed with blood of fathers, mothers etc. Hml. II, 2, 479 (Dyce: 'this is properly an heraldic term, == blazoned, trick meaning a delineation of arms, in which the colours are distinguished by their technical marks, without any colour being laid on').
